introSection: Le Vampire, or Vampire, is a steel inverted roller coaster of Bolliger & Mabillard built in 2002 located in La Ronde, Montréal, Québec, Canada. The onsite construction of the ride was done by Martin & Vleminckx. It is also a mirrored Batman clone.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In February 2002, La Ronde announced that they would be spending $16.7 million on new attractions, such as Vampire.

Vampire opened on 18 May 2002.

In 2016, Vampire ran with one train facing backwards.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: After climbing the chain lift hill, the train goes into a vertical loop, a zero-g roll, another vertical loop, a helix, and two corkscrews before entering into the brakes section.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Le Vampire has a yellow track and purple supports.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: A 67-year-old park employee was struck and killed by the ride in July 2012 after entering a restricted area.

fullText:
Le Vampire, or Vampire, is a steel inverted roller coaster of Bolliger & Mabillard built in 2002 located in La Ronde, Montréal, Québec, Canada. The onsite construction of the ride was done by Martin & Vleminckx. It is also a mirrored Batman clone.

In February 2002, La Ronde announced that they would be spending $16.7 million on new attractions, such as Vampire.

Vampire opened on 18 May 2002.

In 2016, Vampire ran with one train facing backwards.

After climbing the chain lift hill, the train goes into a vertical loop, a zero-g roll, another vertical loop, a helix, and two corkscrews before entering into the brakes section.

Le Vampire has a yellow track and purple supports.



A 67-year-old park employee was struck and killed by the ride in July 2012 after entering a restricted area.
